title: Projects
text:
This project examined whether remote jobs were increasing or decreasing across industries in 2024 using the Lightcast job postings dataset. The analysis compared the share of job postings labeled as remote across major industries from May to September 2024.
Instead of focusing only on total job posting volume, the project measured the percentage of remote postings within each industry. This made it possible to compare remote hiring trends across industries of different sizes.
Research Question:
Are remote jobs increasing or decreasing across industries?
Key Findings:

Remote job trends did not move in one uniform direction across industries.
Some industries showed an increase in remote job share between May and September 2024.
General Medical and Surgical Hospitals, Unclassified Industry, and All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services had some of the strongest increases.
Several computer-related industries showed declines in remote job share, including Custom Computer Programming Services, Computer Systems Design Services, and Other Computer Related Services.
The results suggest that remote-work opportunities should be understood as industry-specific rather than as one general labor-market trend.

Tools Used:

Python
pandas
matplotlib
Quarto
GitHub Pages

Skills Demonstrated:

Data cleaning
Feature classification
Industry-level trend analysis
Time-series comparison
Data visualization
Professional web reporting





This project applied machine learning techniques in a cloud computing environment. The work included feature engineering, model training, and model evaluation using scalable tools.
Tools Used:

AWS EC2
PySpark
Python
Quarto
GitHub

Skills Demonstrated:

Cloud-based analytics workflow
Scalable machine learning
Regression modeling
Feature importance analysis
Technical reporting





This team project evaluated financial and strategic decisions for a proposed microbrewery. The analysis included break-even analysis, sensitivity analysis, risk analysis, and optimization analysis.
Tools Used:

Excel
Financial modeling
Business analytics frameworks
Presentation design

Skills Demonstrated:

Break-even analysis
Sensitivity analysis
Risk analysis
Business decision-making
Strategic communication

title: Zichen Zhang
text:
Hi, I’m Zichen Zhang, a graduate student in Applied Business Analytics at Boston University Metropolitan College.
I am interested in business analytics, cloud computing, data visualization, machine learning, and decision-making analytics. Through my coursework and projects, I have developed experience using Python, SQL, PySpark, AWS, GitHub, Quarto, and data visualization tools.
This portfolio website showcases my academic projects, technical skills, and professional development.


My academic background includes courses and projects related to:

Business analytics
Cloud analytics
Data mining
Machine learning
Decision-making analytics
Data visualization
Web analytics





Python
SQL
R
PySpark
AWS EC2
Git and GitHub
Quarto
pandas
matplotlib
Data visualization




My goal is to pursue opportunities in business analytics, data analytics, or cloud analytics. I hope to use data-driven methods to support business decision-making and communicate insights clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

title: CV
text:
You can download my CV here:
Download Zichen Zhang CV


I am a graduate student in Applied Business Analytics at Boston University Metropolitan College, with academic training in business analytics, data science, mathematics, cloud analytics, and machine learning.
My experience includes data analysis, database organization, spreadsheet-based reporting, MATLAB-based data preprocessing, Linux-based data collection, and applied analytics projects.



Boston University, Metropolitan College
M.S. in Applied Business Analytics
Boston, MA
Expected: December 2026
North China University of Technology and Brunel University London
B.S. in Mathematics for Data Science
Data Science and Big Data Technology Honorary Bachelor of Science Degree
Graduated: July 2025



Data Analyst Intern — TSINO-TEK Beijing Co. Ltd.
August 2024 – September 2024

Extracted and reorganized raw production-line data from an SMT database for downstream data analysis.
Used MATLAB and Excel to preprocess production and dispatch data.
Compared customer order demand with inventory data to identify mismatches and help prevent supply chain issues.

Full-Time Software Front-end Engineer — Open Bayes
January 2024 – March 2024

Practiced Java and programming problem-solving during onboarding.
Collected and integrated large-scale model training data using Linux terminal tools.
Processed QA dataset files and documented data sources, formats, and processing steps.




Tesla ADAS Performance Analytics
Student Consultant — Boston University Open Avenues Foundation & Forensic Rock

Analyzed FCW and AEB system performance using test measurement data.
Built visual analytics comparing trigger timing, distance, speed, TTC, acceleration, and braking behavior.
Evaluated system robustness and identified performance limitations under offset pedestrian tests.

Regional Data Collection and Ecological Investigation of Water Quality

Conducted field research at Miyun Water Reservoir with 500+ survey participants.
Visualized survey and water-resource data using MATLAB and R.
Presented findings to local government and received project awards.
